search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk CG32 unintentional weight loss appetite intake screening
site:nice.org.uk NG12 weight loss gastrointestinal symptoms colorectal upper GI
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic cancer, malnutrition, malabsorption, endocrine, psychiatric, or infection diagnosis
automatic blood test, imaging, endoscopy, supplement, diet, admission, or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Weight loss is nonspecific and requires measured trajectory and broad assessment.
No diagnosis, test, or management is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults with unintentional weight loss and possible GI context; children, pregnancy, eating disorders, acute severe illness, known cancer, and end-of-life contexts require separate evidence.
setting_applicability: Primary, gastroenterology, hepatology, colorectal, result-review, or outpatient follow-up as qualified by the exact source.
UAE_applicability: International evidence requires UAE gastroenterology, colorectal, hepatology, laboratory, endoscopy, referral, prescribing, screening, and local-pathway adaptation.
recency_verification: Exact official documents and sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The cited official source remains current on the issuing organisation website at the review date.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact screening section supports duration, measured or reported weight loss, appetite, intake, swallowing, bowel symptoms, illness, and functional impact without assigning malnutrition or cause.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting unintentional weight loss, appetite, taste, swallowing, bowel habit, prolonged illness, BMI, percentage weight loss, reduced intake and future intake risk when actually assessed.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact colorectal section supports clinician assessment of bowel-habit change, rectal bleeding, abdominal symptoms, anaemia, and weight-loss concern without diagnosing cancer.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician recognition of rectal bleeding, change in bowel habit, abdominal pain, weight loss, anaemia, and rectal-mass contexts with recommendation-specific age qualifiers; it does not assert cancer.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact section supports recording existing laboratory, imaging, endoscopy, nutrition-risk, and clinician-entered plan context without generating tests or nutrition support.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-recorded malnutrition or risk criteria and consent or best-interest discussion; no diagnosis, calculation, feeding route, or treatment is generated.

unresolved_source_gaps: Measured weight, percentage and timeframe, intake, swallowing, bowel symptoms, systemic context, cause, diagnosis, tests, nutrition support, referral, urgency, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
